sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The block moved from (34, 18) to (32, 20). It moved up 2 and right 2. So the block seems to move toward the clicked position, by about 2 cells in each direction.
Now the block is at (32, 20), and I need to get it to (27, 33). That's about 5 rows up and 13 columns right. I'll need multiple clicks.
But wait - I have 4 blocks to move, and I have a limited action budget. Let me think about the most efficient way to move all blocks to the center.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the blocks don't all need to go to the exact same spot. Maybe they need to form a pattern around the blue blob, or maybe they need to touch the blue blob.
Let me try to move all 4 blocks toward the center more aggressively. I'll click closer to the target position to make bigger jumps.
Actually, let me first understand the movement better. When I clicked (32, 20) and the block was at (34, 18), it moved to (32, 20) - exactly to the clicked position! So the block jumps directly to the clicked position (or close to it).
Let me verify: the block moved from center (34.5, 18.5) to (32, 20). The click was at (32, 20). The new center is (32, 20) which is exactly where I clicked!
So the mechanic is: clicking on an empty cell moves the nearest magenta block to that position (the block's center becomes the clicked position).
This is much simpler! I can just click on the target positions directly.
Now, where should I move the blocks? Let me think about the goal. The blue blob is at (27, 33). Maybe I need to move all 4 blocks to converge on the blob, or to specific positions around it.
Let me try moving one block directly to the blue blob's center and see what happens.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The block moved from (34, 18) to (32, 20) - exactly to where I clicked!
# So clicking on an empty cell moves the nearest magenta block to that position.

# Let me try moving a block directly to the blue blob's center (27, 33)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 33}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
